title: BEA 2026 Release Schedule - U.S. Economic Data Calendar
summary: The U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis published its 2026 release schedule for key economic indicators including GDP estimates, Personal Income and Outlays, and International Trade data. All listed releases are scheduled for 8:30 AM Eastern time. The schedule contains no actual economic data, only future release dates for government statistics. This is a government calendar page with minimal standalone archival value.
key_points: GDP releases scheduled quarterly in 2026: Advance (January, April, July, October), Second Estimate (February, May, August, November), and Third Estimate (March, June, September, December)
Personal Income and Outlays reports scheduled monthly at 8:30 AM
U.S. International Trade in Goods and Services released monthly at 8:30 AM
Annual updates for Foreign Direct Investment (June) and U.S. Multinational Enterprises (November) included
State-level economic data including State GDP and State Personal Income released with quarterly GDP third estimates
No numerical data included in this source; only release calendar with dates and data types
why_it_matters: This schedule serves as a macro catalyst calendar for traders and analysts tracking U.S. economic data releases. BEA data (GDP, personal income, trade) are market-moving events that can influence equity, bond, and currency markets. The standardized 8:30 AM release time allows market participants to prepare for high-impact data releases. However, this page itself provides no analytical content and should be referenced contextually rather than archived as a standalone record.
macro_context: BEA releases are among the most important U.S. economic indicators. GDP data provides comprehensive view of economic activity; Personal Income and Outlays reports affect consumption and inflation expectations; International Trade data affects current account analysis. This page only provides timing information, not actual data values.

issues_found: Source contains no actual economic data, only future release dates and data type labels
PUBLISHED_AT field is empty - no publication date available
PAGE_TYPE metadata says 'data_release' but actual content is a release schedule/calendar, not a data release
Extremely thin content with no analytical value or numerical evidence
Limited standalone archive value - useful only as a reference tool
Source is a government calendar page, not an article or substantive content piece

notes: This is a government release calendar, not actual economic data. While relevant to macro catalyst tracking, the source provides no numerical content—only dates and data type categories. Archive value is moderate as a reference tool for scheduled economic releases. Confidence reduced because source is thin on substantive content and lacks published_at timestamp. Consider archiving alongside actual BEA data releases for context.
